name: ReadingFrame
mission: Monthly, hype-free breakdowns of biotech and AI research — methods, limitations, and what findings actually mean — from a high school researcher in Bentonville, Arkansas.

author.name: Heram Nagabhairu
author.bio: I'm a high school researcher interested in ML, bioinformatics, and computational biology. ReadingFrame is where I write about one paper or development a month — without the press release layer.
author.longBio: I got into ML and bioinformatics a few years ago — graph neural networks, gene expression, computational approaches to problems that are still genuinely hard. Mostly Python, Google Colab, GitHub. No university affiliation. One post a month. No ads, no affiliate links.

newsletter.provider: Mailchimp or Substack — replace action URL in content/site.json

categories.name: Biotech
categories.description: Gene editing, therapeutics, and the systems that move discoveries from lab to clinic.

categories.name: CRISPR
categories.description: Delivery, safety, and clinical translation of programmable genome editing.

categories.name: AI & ML
categories.description: Machine learning in drug discovery, biomedicine, and biological data analysis.

categories.name: Neuroscience
categories.description: Brain-computer interfaces and neural technology with real trial data.

categories.name: De-Extinction
categories.description: Engineered organisms, conservation claims, and what the science actually shows.
